##NAMES=Cuatrocapaite-(NH4)
##RRUFFID=R250040
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(NH_4_)_3_(NaMg[box])(As_2_O_3_)_6_Cl_6_·16H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Vermiform stacks and in satin-spar-like vein fillings of transparent hexagonal plates associated with cuatrocapaite-(K). Type sample data from Kampf et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250040
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(NH_4_)_2.48_Na_1.66_Mg_0.87_K_0.09_(As_12_O_18.05_)Cl_5.88_·16.02H_2_O ; N was analysed by gas chromatography. H_2_O was calculated from the idealised formula
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.2501 b: 5.2501 c: 46.639 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 1113.3 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
